text: Robadi left middle lobectomy in a patient with situs inversus totalis. Structure of the middle lobe bronchus is long and narrow. And also, lobar isolation for middle lobe causes inadequate collateral ventilation. Patients who have repeated episodes of infection with lobar collapse and evidence of bronchocytosis, tumor or obstruction from a large lymph node can be defined as middle lobe syndrome. Treatment of this pathology is surgical resection.
Chest CT of the patient is compatible with middle lobe syndrome. Isolation of the middle lobe between upper and lower lobes.

The surgeon opens the anterior mediastinal pleura and dissects the superior pulmonary vein, isolating the middle lobe vein. The fissure is dissected, followed by the lymph node compressing the middle lobe bronchus. Using conventional Watts instruments, the middle lobe bronchus is encircled and dissected. The middle lobe artery is encircled, hemoclips are placed, and the artery is divided with scissors.
The surgeon completes the lobectomy procedure by firing multiple stapler devices to resect the middle lobe bronchus.
